seoTitle: How to Create Stories With Atlassian Intelligence
metaDescription: Learn how to use Atlassian Intelligence to generate user stories and acceptance criteria in Jira, then refine the AI output for your team.
intro: Writing consistent, well-structured user stories from scratch takes time, and Atlassian Intelligence can help speed up that process. This guide covers generating AI-powered stories in Jira, creating acceptance criteria, and editing the output before it goes into your backlog.

steps.heading: Understand what Atlassian Intelligence offers
steps.body: Atlassian Intelligence uses AI to help generate structured content like stories and acceptance criteria directly inside Jira.

steps.heading: Generate AI user stories
steps.body: Use the AI prompt within Jira to generate a draft story based on a short description of what you need.

steps.heading: Edit and improve the stories
steps.body: Review the AI-generated draft and refine the wording, scope, and acceptance criteria so it matches your team's actual needs.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for AI story creation
steps.body: Treat AI output as a starting point rather than a final answer, and have a product owner or Scrum Master review it before it enters the backlog.

faq.question: How do I create stories using Atlassian Intelligence?
faq.answer: Use the AI prompt inside Jira's issue creation flow to generate a draft story, then review and edit it before saving.

faq.question: Can Atlassian Intelligence write acceptance criteria too?
faq.answer: Yes, it can generate acceptance criteria alongside the story description, which you can then refine to match your team's standards.

faq.question: Should I always edit AI-generated stories before using them?
faq.answer: Yes, AI-generated stories should be reviewed and adjusted to make sure they're accurate and specific to your actual project needs.

faq.question: Who should review AI-generated stories before sprint planning?
faq.answer: Product owners and Scrum Masters are well positioned to review AI-generated stories for accuracy and completeness before planning.
